---

# `pick` Function

Keep only specified keys from a map or object

Returns a new map or object containing only the keys listed in 'keys'. Keys that do not exist in the input are silently ignored.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
pick(input dynamic, keys list of string) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The map or object to select keys from.
1. `keys` (List of String) The keys to keep.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->


## Example Usage

### Basic usage

```terraform
locals {
  entry = { env = "prod", region = "europe-west1", service = { id = "api", port = 8080 } }
}

output "identity_attrs" {
  value = provider::util::pick(local.entry, ["env", "service"])
}
# Result: { env = "prod", service = { id = "api", port = 8080 } }
```

### Using with combine and for_each

A common pattern is to use `pick` to extract only the attributes needed for a resource name or tag set from a `combine` result.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::combine([
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
    { key = "tier",   items = ["api", "web"] },
  ])
}

resource "google_compute_network" "vpc" {
  for_each = provider::util::filter(local.all, { env = "prod" })
  name     = "vpc-${each.value.env}-${each.value.region}"
  # Pass only the subset of attributes this resource needs as labels
  labels   = provider::util::pick(each.value, ["env", "region"])
}
```
